testing_centers.notes: In-person test centers worldwide and online proctored via OnVUE

id_requirements: Two forms of ID are required. Primary ID must be government-issued, include candidate's name, photo, and signature (e.g. passport, driver's license). Secondary ID must include candidate's name and signature or name and photo.
exam_rules: No notes, scratch paper, electronic devices, books, or study materials permitted. Personal items must be stored. No food or drink except water. Restroom breaks allowed but timer continues. Testing center staff may monitor via video/audio.
renewal_options: Earn CE credits through CompTIA CE program (training, conferences, publishing, teaching, higher certs) or retake the current exam version before expiry.
recommended_experience: equivalent to 6–12 months of hands-on experience managing projects in a tech environment

retake_policy.notes: No waiting period required before first retake. After the second failed attempt, candidates must wait 14 calendar days before any subsequent attempt. No limit on total attempts.

objectives.title: Project characteristics and methodologies
sub_objectives: Explaining project characteristics, methodologies, and frameworks.

objectives.title: Agile vs. waterfall
sub_objectives: Comparing criteria for selection, team composition, and communication methods.

objectives.title: Change control
sub_objectives: Applying project-specific change control and management processes.

objectives.title: Risk management
sub_objectives: Identifying risks, analyzing responses, and understanding roles and responsibilities.

objectives.title: Issue management
sub_objectives: Tracking issues, resolving them, and documenting outcomes.

objectives.title: Schedule management
sub_objectives: Identifying milestones, sequencing activities, estimating resources, and maintaining schedules.

objectives.title: Quality and performance management
sub_objectives: Comparing retrospectives, sprint reviews, service-level agreements (SLAs), key performance indicators (KPIs), audits, and testing cycles.

objectives.title: Communication management
sub_objectives: Assessing methods, developing platforms, and managing communication.

objectives.title: Meeting management
sub_objectives: Setting agendas, assigning roles, timeboxing, and documenting action items.

objectives.title: Team and resource management
sub_objectives: Analyzing resource gaps, managing team performance, and defining roles.

objectives.title: Procurement and vendor selection
sub_objectives: Evaluating vendors, contracts, and procurement methods.

objectives.title: Discovery phase artifacts
sub_objectives: Explaining business cases, prequalified vendors, and financial concepts.

objectives.title: Project initiation
sub_objectives: Developing project charters, identifying stakeholders, creating responsibility assignment matrices (RAMs), and conducting kickoff meetings.

objectives.title: Project planning
sub_objectives: Assigning resources, developing communication plans, defining scope, creating schedules, and performing risk assessments.

objectives.title: Project execution
sub_objectives: Managing tasks, vendors, meetings, budgets, conflicts, and phase gate reviews.

objectives.title: Project closing
sub_objectives: Validating deliverables, closing contracts, releasing resources, archiving documentation, and conducting closure meetings.

objectives.title: Project tools
sub_objectives: Using Gantt charts, burndown charts, PERT (program evaluation review technique) charts, issue logs, change logs, risk registers, dashboards, and time-tracking tools.

objectives.title: Productivity tools
sub_objectives: Comparing communication, collaboration, meeting, documentation, scheduling, and ticketing tools.

objectives.title: Quality and performance charts
sub_objectives: Analyzing histograms, Pareto charts, scatter diagrams, fishbone diagrams, burnup/burndown charts, and decision trees.

objectives.title: Environmental, social, and governance (ESG)
sub_objectives: Summarizing project impacts on the environment, regulations, company values, and brand value.

objectives.title: Information security
sub_objectives: Explaining physical, operational, digital, and data security concepts.

objectives.title: Compliance and privacy
sub_objectives: Understanding confidentiality, legal impacts, and privacy regulations.

objectives.title: IT concepts
sub_objectives: Summarizing infrastructure, cloud models, and software basics.

objectives.title: Change control
sub_objectives: Explaining IT infrastructure change control, software change control, CI/CD (continuous integration/continuous deployment), and production vs. staging environments.

official_study_resources.title: CertMaster Learn
official_study_resources.notes: Official e-learning with performance-based questions and study guides

official_study_resources.title: CertMaster Practice
official_study_resources.notes: Adaptive practice question engine with rationale
